story.title: Larry Shotten Oral History
story.description: The interview delves into personal and communal histories, primarily centering on experiences in Bloomfield and reflections on identity, culture, and societal changes. The interviewee, growing up in a Jewish family, stresses the significance of cultural understanding and inclusivity, noting Bloomfield's transformation over decades. They highlight the town's designation as an "All-American City," its diverse population, and structural changes, such as the rise of apartment complexes. The respondent recalls experiences in local education systems, contrasting past and current challenges, and expresses concern about maintaining educational standards while acknowledging Bloomfield's efforts to support its senior citizens.
